note: Chart-provenance task. The executor gets a cropped chart image (sources/crop.jpg) with the TITLE and the SOURCE/footer removed. They must identify what the chart shows and trace it to the canonical chart page AND its underlying data source. The crop keeps the curves, country labels (China/US/India/Germany/Brazil/UK), and the y-axis units ('billion t'), but not the title or the data-source citation — so the executor must reconstruct provenance, not read it off the image.

crop_origin.removed: top title band ('Annual CO₂ emissions') and bottom source/footer band (the 'Data source: ...' line)

canonical_answer.what_it_shows: annual total CO₂ emissions by country/region over time, in tonnes (excludes land-use change)
canonical_answer.underlying_data_source: Global Carbon Budget (2025) / Global Carbon Project — 'Global Carbon Budget v15', with major processing by Our World in Data

scoring_anchors.must_identify: the chart shows annual CO₂ emissions by country over time
scoring_anchors.must_name_underlying_source: the Global Carbon Budget / Global Carbon Project (processed by OWID) — this is the key provenance step the cropped-out footer hid
scoring_anchors.primary_source_rule: canonical = the OWID grapher page + its cited data provider (Global Carbon Project). A random news article reusing the chart, or just saying 'some CO2 chart', is insufficient.
scoring_anchors.tolerance: The exact Global Carbon Budget YEAR/version (2025 / v15) may differ as OWID updates; accept naming the Global Carbon Project / Global Carbon Budget as the underlying source even if the version differs. Full credit = correct OWID grapher page + Global Carbon Project as the data origin.
